description: Sources the narrative system watches. Each entry is a place that produces public-facing or pitch-shaped content. The watch script reads new content from each source and produces ingest digests for review.

sources.description: Long-form essays ingested into Tractorpedia raw — Building Revolution, Cure Already Exists, etc.

The five constellation files that make up the JusticeHub program, now grouped under wiki/projects/justicehub/: justicehub (platform), the-full-idea (STAY comprehensive), three-circles (Minderoo pitch), staying (methodology layer), the-brave-ones (photographic art). These are LIVE strategic documents — re-ingest weekly so claim files stay aligned with edits.
Working handoffs for the STAY journal and program — locked models, visual language, session handoffs. Internal-only, never public.
Locked CONTAINED campaign material — campaign bible, identity, launch posts, partner outreach, oped drafts.
Personal and broadcast email drafts — funder, ally, decision-maker outreach.
LinkedIn comments + reactions on CONTAINED posts. Each entry becomes a candidate audience reaction.
Goods on Country strategic docs — unit cost reports, community ownership checkpoints, buyer pipeline.
Source content for the live CONTAINED site (justicehub.com.au/contained).
Articles published on the JusticeHub website + ACT website. Use Supabase MCP to query. Rows tagged by project_code map to the project field automatically.
Empathy Ledger stories. Storyteller quotes become candidate audience reactions or testimonial variants. Respect consent_scope before promoting any quote into a public claim.
Email responses to outreach in GHL CRM — these are first-party audience reactions to deployed claims. Match by date + recipient against the corresponding claim.
Public website for A Curious Tractor. Scrape periodically to detect new pages, copy changes, and project descriptions.
Live JusticeHub site. The /contained subroute is the CONTAINED public surface.

schedules.weekly: Run via cron at 06:00 AEST every Monday
schedules.manual: Run only when triggered by /wiki narrative ingest <source-id>

notes: Sources marked `wired: false` are configured but not yet implemented in narrative-watch.mjs. The local-folder sources work today; the API/scrape sources need adapters.
Project: 'auto' means the source contains content for multiple projects — the ingest script will use the file content (project_code, slug, tags) to route into the right narrative folder.

Source type hints are passed to narrative-ingest.mjs so the digest can frame each finding correctly (a LinkedIn comment is treated as an audience reaction by default; a pitch document is treated as candidate claims; an EL story is treated as a testimonial variant).
Add new sources here. The watch script reads this file at every run.
